title: HIKET workshop brought together Finnish and Swedish researchers and experts in Helsinki
content:
A big thank you to all participants for the engaging conversations and valuable insights! We explored current developments, challenges and opportunities related to land-use carbon balance monitoring, greenhouse gas inventories, modelling and data integration.

The discussions highlighted the value of bringing together different perspectives and expertise to address the complex scientific questions surrounding land-use sector carbon balances.

The conversations don't end here - we look forward to continuing the collaboration and exploring new opportunities to work together in the future.

title: New HEPP WPS publication: Reflections on HEPP5
content:
We are now very happy to announce the publication of the fifth volume of the HEPP WPS, . With insightful contributions written and edited after HEPP5, the volume truly captures the breadth of topics connected to emotions, polarisation, and populism that all HEPP participants have come to expect from our events. The articles break down into four categories: theoretical contributions, media approaches, global case studies, and one history piece.  

The opportunities that the HEPP conferences offer don’t stop once the event ends. All participants have the opportunity to publish a piece based on their conference contribution in the , which we launched after HEPP2 in 2021. The HEPP2 conference was held fully online in 2021, and the organizers have opted for a hybrid format ever since.

title: Welcoming new Visiting Fellows for 2026-2027
content:
Claudia Eggart 
Lived Geopolitics: Re-scaling Market Infrastructures from Soviet Collapse to Imperial Backlash 
Leibniz-Zentrum Moderner Orient, Germany 

Kristen Ghodsee 
The Gaslighting of Eastern Europe: How International Financial Institutions Disappeared the 20th Century’s Second Great Depression 
University of Pennsylvania, USA

James Paul Goode 
War, Media, and Russian Political Migrants
Carleton University , Canada

Galina Miazhevich 
Feminism in Turbulent Times: Evidence from Ukraine, Russia and Poland
Cardiff University, United Kingdom

Svitlana Pavlenko 
Scientific-Technical Contacts as Channels of Espionage: KGB Industrial Intelligence in Dnipropetrovsk, 1950s–1960s
Centre for Advanced Study, Sofia, Bulgaria

More details on each of these research projects will be available on our website in early August. The Visiting Fellows will present their work in seminars during 2026-2027, and you are warmly invited to attend.

title: Alaa Kadhim joins our group as MSc thesis student
content:
Alaa Kadhim has joined our research group as an MSc thesis student in Pharmaceutical Research, Development and Safety. Her thesis focuses on using stimulated Raman scattering (SRS) microscopy to study the behaviour of amorphous drugs in tablet formulations, with particular emphasis on how excipients influence drug stability and crystallisation. Alaa has a background in chemistry and materials science, supporting her interdisciplinary approach to pharmaceutical research.

title: Conference greetings: Current Advances in Gambling Research
content:
Centre for Research on Addiction, Control and Governance had the pleasure of hosting this year’s Current Advances in Gambling Research conference at the University of Helsinki. 

CAGR 2026 included almost a hundred oral and poster presentations from 21 different countries. The multidisciplinary conference offered perspectives on gambling research from different countries and fields, including lived experience, clinicians, and regulators. 

Three days included a wide selection of research evidence on gambling harms among individuals and communities, effective gambling regulations and interventions, research on industry tactics, as well as critical reflection and new possibilities for gambling research. 

Ingeborg Rossow (Norwegian Institute of Public Health) opened the conference with a keynote that examined how the total consumption model, based on population level approach to alcohol consumption, can be applied to gambling.
snippet: Centre for Research on Addiction, Control and Governance had the pleasure of hosting this year’s Current Advances in Gambling Research conference at the University of Helsinki.
creator: kmette@helsinki.fi

title: The Anders Jahre Prize for Young Scientists to Heidi Haikala
content:
Heidi Haikala, who works at the University of Helsinki, receives the Anders Jahre Prize for Young Scientists for her work on mechanisms of cancer drugs – what makes the drugs stop working so that this problem can be prevented and to find new targets for therapy.

– I am extremely grateful to receive the Anders Jahre Prize for Young Scientists. While the award is given to an individual, the work behind it reflects the efforts of many people. I have been fortunate to be surrounded by talented colleagues, mentors, students, and collaborators who have challenged and reshaped my thinking. This recognition is encouraging for our mission to tackle drug resistance and improve outcomes for cancer patients. I appreciate the opportunity to continue building on this work together with our exceptional team, says Haikala.

title: Greetings from bi-annual CBRU seminar
content:
On Friday, May 29th, CBRU members gathered in Biomedicum, Meilahti for a spring seminar. Five CBRU alumni, from professors to entrepreneurs, shared their career paths to help junior researchers in planning their own future. A warm thank you to all the speakers and participants!

Perjantaina 29.5. järjestettiin CBRU:n kevätseminaari Biomedicumissa Meilahdessa. Viisi entistä CBRU:laista professoreista yrittäjiin kertoi urapolustaan ja auttoi nuoria tutkijoita pohtimaan omaa tulevaisuuttaan. Lämmin kiitos kaikille puhujille ja osallistujille!

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

A temporary bridge for pedestrians will be built on the east side of Arkadiankatu over the Baana. The aim is to maintain the pedestrian route on the east side of the bridge until the temporary bridge is operational. Cyclists will also be able to use the bridge.

The Baana route underneath the bridge is expected to remain open for most of the renovation's duration, with some minor exceptions.

There will be changes to the motor vehicle routes by the bridge and around it in January. If possible, should be favoured during the bridge renovation.

Connections to the bus terminals in Kamppi will remain available, but there will be a slight diversion of routes.

Trams 1 and 1T will run via Simonkatu and Kamppi. Lasipalatsi and the Natural History Museum will not be served.

The diversion is due to the renovation of the Arkadiankatu bridge and will remain in place until late 2026. The renovation will not affect HSL's bus services. 

Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13

title: Ukrainan sota - yliopiston suositukset
content:
The University of Helsinki has condemned the military action by Russia and expressed its support for Ukraine and its university community. We have also expressed our support for Ukraine, together with other Finnish universities, through statements by and the . As a demonstration of support, we have illuminated buildings in the colours of the Ukrainian flag and raised the flag on our premises.

One of the principles of the University of Helsinki is to promote international collaboration in research and higher education, and to support these efforts even in difficult circumstances. Russia’s indefensible military aggression in Ukraine has required exceptional measures, which include those taken in the collaboration between universities.

The University of Helsinki is monitoring both national and EU-level sanctions, adapting our policies and processes accordingly. Together with other Finnish and international universities, the University of Helsinki strives to promote the end of military action in Ukraine, while supporting Ukrainian institutions and people.

title: Esko Karvinen will defend his PhD thesis
content:
Esko Karvinen will defend his doctoral thesis entitled “Biogenic carbon cycling dynamics in diverse urban green spaces” at the University of Helsinki on June 16, 2026 at 12.00.

The public examination will take place in auditorium 108 (B3) at the Forest Sciences Building, University of Helsinki (Latokartanonkaari 7, Helsinki). The Opponent is Associate Professor Andreas Ibrom, Technical University of Denmark, and the Custos is professor Jaana Bäck.

The book is available in Helda:
